title: var, let, const
description: Learn the three ways to declare variables in JavaScript and when to use each.

blocks.content:
In JavaScript, you declare a variable using one of three keywords: var, let, or const. A variable is a named container that holds a value.

var is the oldest way to declare a variable. It was the only option before 2015. Today it is rarely used in modern code because it has some surprising behaviours around scope.

let and const were introduced in ES6 (2015) and are now the standard.

Use let when you expect the value to change. Use const when the value should stay fixed after it is first assigned.

question.question: Which keyword should you use when a value will not change?
var, let, and const are all used to ___ variables.
Order these keywords from oldest to newest introduction in JavaScript.
